context: Evaluates the implementation of a Tauri command that creates and persists a new notebook. The command must assign a UUIDv7 identifier and timestamps, accept an optional parent_id for nested hierarchy, and write the notebook as JSON to the correct file path. Proper use of the vsyncnotes Notebook struct and FsRepo persistence layer is assessed.

checklist.name: Notebook struct usage
checklist.description: Constructs a `Notebook` value using the expected fields: `id` (UUIDv7), `title`, `parent_id` (Option<Uuid>), `sort_order` (defaulting to 0), `created_at`, and `updated_at`.

checklist.name: UUIDv7 generation
checklist.description: Assigns a new UUIDv7 identifier to the notebook using `uuid::Uuid::now_v7()` or the equivalent v7 generation API, not v4 or a static value.

checklist.name: Timestamp assignment
checklist.description: Sets both `created_at` and `updated_at` to the current UTC time at creation; both fields should be equal on a freshly created notebook.

checklist.name: Optional parent_id
checklist.description: Accepts an `Option<Uuid>` for `parent_id` and stores it correctly in the `Notebook` struct, enabling hierarchical notebook organization.

checklist.name: FsRepo persistence
checklist.description: Calls `FsRepo::save_notebook()` (or equivalent) to write the notebook to `notebooks/{uuid}.json` within the vault directory.

checklist.name: Tauri command return
checklist.description: Returns the created `Notebook` struct (not just an ID) on success, and a `String` error on failure, following the `Result<Notebook, String>` pattern.
